objective: Act as a careful researcher; use web/CLI/remote evidence; inspect data; find methodology bugs; and try to break T1/T3 CCC ceilings.

checks.requirement: Act slowly/analytically and follow repo leakage discipline
Use web search / current SOTA context
Use Kimi, Claude, GLMCode, and Gemini where available

evidence.persisted_evidence: thread_goal_completion_audit_20260508.md records Kimi/Gemini use plus Claude low-credit and glmcode unavailable.

checks.requirement: Use remote server and inspect utilization
Create logs/visualizations of the best T1 and T3 pipelines
Keep artifact, claim-labeling, and reproducibility guards current

warnings.message: Shared helpers intentionally return 0.0 for fewer than three finite pairs; headline vectors are unaffected.

historical_boundary.heading: # ACTIVE MISSION — iter26 Hssayeni MJFF Acquisition (2026-05-05 PM) — BLOCKED at Synapse DUA gate (F62)

decision.reason: The best diagnostic CCC lift is small and non-reportable; variance matching raises CCC by +0.0212 but worsens MAE by +1.1390.

decision.summary: No single-subject influence pattern justifies a model update. T3 max |leave-one CCC delta| is 0.0381; T1 iter34-minus-iter12 matched delta stays positive under all leave-one deletions (minimum 0.0629). Influence is still severity-tail concentrated, so this is a claim-fragility caveat rather than a filtering rule.

Current iter47 T3 residuals are most associated with unobservable_non_gait (r=-0.8004). The best single-domain privileged oracle is unobservable_non_gait (delta CCC +0.4716); the best gait-observable proxy is gait_balance_7_14 (delta CCC +0.2083). Because these corrections use true clinical domain labels, they are explanation only.

checks.requirement: Sit with the data and derive methodology fixes

warnings.interpretation: companion array has full 94-slot cohort shape; JSON reports valid target/evaluation count.

decision.recommended_next: Do not launch another WearGait-only scalar feature addition. Use this audit to justify either data access/restoration or a fresh preregistered target-representation hypothesis.

guardrail.reason: Second-level transforms are fit on the saved OOF vector. For a held-out subject i, the calibration set includes predictions for other subjects generated by base models whose training folds included subject i. This is acceptable as a diagnostic but not as a reportable nested meta-model.
guardrail.future_reportable_requirement: A reportable rescaling/meta-calibration would need outer-fold base predictions and inner-OOF calibration predictions generated without the outer test subject.

decision.reason: The best diagnostic CCC lift is small and non-reportable; variance matching raises CCC by +0.0212 but worsens MAE by +1.1390.

decision.summary: No single-subject influence pattern justifies a model update. T3 max |leave-one CCC delta| is 0.0381; T1 iter34-minus-iter12 matched delta stays positive under all leave-one deletions (minimum 0.0629). Influence is still severity-tail concentrated, so this is a claim-fragility caveat rather than a filtering rule.

Current iter47 T3 residuals are most associated with unobservable_non_gait (r=-0.8004). The best single-domain privileged oracle is unobservable_non_gait (delta CCC +0.4716); the best gait-observable proxy is gait_balance_7_14 (delta CCC +0.2083). Because these corrections use true clinical domain labels, they are explanation only.

decision.recommended_next: Do not launch another WearGait-only T3 scalar-feature or calibration screen. The residual is item/domain target anatomy, not an uncovered deployable feature block.

checks.requirement: Attempt to break T1 CCC ceiling
Attempt to break T3 CCC ceiling
Evaluate public external routes and avoid dead-end repeats

paradigma_yin.name: ParaDigMa open PD digital biomarker toolbox
rationale: ParaDigMa is a feature-extraction toolbox (arm swing, tremor, pulse rate from wrist IMU), not a new labeled cohort.
Applying it to WearGait would constitute a local scalar handcrafted feature addition.
The repo explicitly closes handcrafted scalar feature additions at N=94 (iter14 FoG-summary scalars NULL on items 9, 12).
T3 IMU feature additions to the iter5 clinical-augmented architecture are also dead (iter6 unsigned-asymmetry NULL, iter6 event-axial NULL).
verify_current_goal_state.py records '0 local model actions remain'.
No orthogonal advantage over already-dead local routes; the N=94 wall is structural (K=500 absorption or gate failure).

paradigma_yin.name: Yin et al Frontiers Neurology 2025 gait-parameter regression
rationale: Raw data is available by author request only. Repo constraint: no scaffold before data/schema for request-only routes.
N=20 PD is below the external augmentation variance floor.
Stronger public routes already tested and failed to advance internal canonicals: FoG-STAR N=22 (iter38 augmentation gate FAIL), COPS N=62 (iter49 partial external validity only).

Input 'gait parameters' are plantar flexion/dorsiflexion angles, stride length, foot clearance, velocity, cadence — likely motion-capture or instrumented-walkway derived, not WearGait-aligned raw wearable IMU.
Small-N LOOCV R2 of 0.675 is not directly comparable to the repo's inductive CCC metric; no fold-clean replication path exists without raw data and schema.

paradigma.name: ParaDigMa PD digital biomarker toolbox
paradigma.access_status: public GitHub/JOSS 2026/Zenodo software; no dataset rows
paradigma.label: No cohort or clinical labels; feature-extraction software only
paradigma.modality: wrist accelerometer, gyroscope, and PPG feature-extraction pipelines during daily-life passive monitoring
paradigma.verdict: Document-only standardized wrist-IMU biomarker toolbox. The route is software, not a labeled cohort; applying it to WearGait would be a local scalar handcrafted feature addition, and that category is already closed at N=94. Do not create a preregistration, scaffold, download job, or remote run for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

yin.name: Yin et al Frontiers Neurology 2025 gait-parameter regression
yin.access_status: paper public; raw data corresponding-author request only; no public row-level schema
yin.label: Total MDS-UPDRS III, tremor part score, and non-tremor part score in OFF/ON medication states
yin.modality: plantar angles, stride length, foot clearance, velocity, cadence, and stride time; likely motion-capture or instrumented-walkway gait parameters rather than raw wearable IMU
yin.verdict: Document-only small-N gait-parameter correlation study. The route is request-only, has no public row-level schema, is underpowered versus already-tested public external rows, and does not expose a WearGait-aligned raw IMU feature path. Do not create an access runbook, preregistration, scaffold, download job, or remote run for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

route.name: Parkinson@Home arm-swing validation
route.access_status: public Radboud WebDAV / DOI route; metadata, clinical CSV, and prepared per-subject parquet files are directly downloadable
route.label: OFF/ON MDS-UPDRS Part III subitems in patient_info.csv; OFF total Part III is computable under valid-range handling
route.modality: bilateral wrist accelerometer and gyroscope at 200 Hz, prepared per-subject parquet files with gait and arm-activity labels
route.verdict: Public direct T3 external route, but iter53 stopped before scoring because the pre-registered feature-readability hard stop retained only 18 valid OFF PD subjects versus the minimum of 20. No zero-shot, within-dataset LOOCV, or OFF/ON response metric exists; no internal WearGait-PD T1/T3 canonical can change. Do not rerun under the same preregistration; any shorter-window or fallback policy requires a fresh preregistration and remains external-validity-only.

consults.kimi: probe_gated_preregistration; run only if public labels/features pass hard stops; no internal canonical update under any outcome

dataset.name: Parkinson@Home IMU sensor data and video annotations for arm swing quantification in free-living gait
dataset.sensor: bilateral wrist accelerometer and gyroscope at 200 Hz
dataset.labels: OFF/ON MDS-UPDRS Part III subitems in patient_info.csv
dataset.cohort: 25 PD with motor fluctuations and 25 controls; PD recorded OFF and ON medication

hard_stops: patient_info.csv must expose all OFF_UPDRS_3_* subitems needed for valid-range T3.
At least 20 PD OFF subjects must remain after valid-target and feature-readability filtering.
Prepared parquet reading must work with pyarrow or fastparquet on the execution host.
External accelerometry scale must be classifiable as g or m/s^2 from magnitude distribution.
No Parkinson@Home labels enter WearGait-trained Tracks A/D.

skipped_subjects.reason: cannot reshape array of size 382 into shape (1,600)
cannot reshape array of size 347 into shape (1,600)
cannot reshape array of size 114 into shape (1,600)
cannot reshape array of size 413 into shape (1,600)

hard_stop.reason: pre-registered minimum of 20 valid OFF PD subjects was not met after right-wrist clean-gait feature extraction

rationale: Parkinson@Home is a material public direct T3 external route: patient_info.csv exposes OFF/ON MDS-UPDRS Part III subitems and the prepared parquet files expose wrist accelerometry with gait/arm labels.
The pre-registered battery required at least 20 valid OFF PD subjects after target and feature-readability filtering before any Track A/C/D score could be reported.
Extraction retained 18 OFF PD subjects, then the hard stop fired before WearGait-to-Parkinson@Home scoring, Parkinson@Home-only LOOCV, or OFF/ON response scoring.
The stopped route must be recorded as no-score/no-canonical-change. A shorter-window or alternate fallback policy would be a new pre-registration, not a continuation of iter53.

consults.kimi: probe_gated_preregistration; run only if public labels/features pass hard stops; no internal canonical update under any outcome

ppmi.name: PPMI / Verily Study Watch
ppmi.access_status: PPMI DUA/application required; no local credentials configured
ppmi.label: MDS-UPDRS Part III and Hoehn & Yahr in PPMI clinical data; npj 2025 analysis linked MDS-UPDRS within 90 days of wearable data
ppmi.modality: Verily Study Watch wrist accelerometer, 100 Hz, free-living longitudinal monitoring
ppmi.verdict: Priority gated direct external T3 route. Kimi advised documenting it and waiting for user PPMI DUA/account action before building a probe scaffold; do not launch a remote job without credentials.

watchpd.name: WATCH-PD
watchpd.access_status: C-Path 3DT Stage 2 membership or WATCH-PD Steering Committee proposal required; no local access or schema; C-Path Integrated Parkinson's Database does not include DHT data
watchpd.label: MDS-UPDRS Parts I-III; APDM sensors worn during MDS-UPDRS Part III; mean PD Part III 24.1 at baseline
watchpd.modality: Apple Watch, iPhone BrainBaseline, and APDM Opal inertial sensors during in-clinic MDS-UPDRS Part III plus at-home longitudinal digital assessments
watchpd.verdict: Direct and protocol-relevant external T3 route, but request-gated and schema-hidden. Add to access queue only; do not create a scaffold, preregistration, download job, or remote run until C-Path 3DT membership or WATCH-PD Steering Committee approval provides row-level sensor files, labels, and schema. PPMI remains higher priority because it is larger and already has a Verily/MDS-UPDRS publication trail.

icicle.name: ICICLE-PD / ICICLE-GAIT
icicle.access_status: request-gated via Newcastle / data available on request; no local agreement or files
icicle.label: MDS-UPDRS Part III and Hoehn & Yahr at clinical visits
icicle.modality: lower-back Axivity AX3 triaxial accelerometer, 100 Hz, +/-8g, 7-day free-living real-world gait at 18-month visits over 6 years
icicle.verdict: Direct longitudinal external T3 route with clinician MDS-UPDRS Part III labels, but request-gated and lower-back rather than wrist-native. Document and pursue access only; do not create a scaffold, preregistration, download job, or remote run until files and schema are available.

cns_portugal.name: CNS Portugal / Lobo IS2022 AX3 gait
cns_portugal.access_status: request-gated through authors/CNS; related CNS Sensors 2022 article states raw data available from authors on request
cns_portugal.label: MDS-UPDRS Part III total and Hoehn & Yahr 2-4
cns_portugal.modality: Axivity AX3 triaxial accelerometers on wrist and lower back, 100 Hz, structured 10-meter walk

published_benchmark.best_10pct_model: Random Forest, 2.5 s window, wrist + lower back
published_benchmark.best_loso_model: SVM, 5 s window, wrist + lower back
published_benchmark.validation_caution: 10% heldout result is window-level/patient-already-seen optimistic; require subject/session-grouped validation

cns_portugal.verdict: Direct request-gated T3 external route with wrist+lower-back AX3 and structured 10 m walk. Do not scaffold before schema and access are confirmed. Treat as external-validity evidence only; not an internal WearGait-PD ceiling-breaker.

mobilised.name: Mobilise-D TVS / CVS
mobilised.access_status: TVS public on Zenodo; CVS row-level public wearable/clinical release or schema not found
mobilised.label: TVS includes demographic/clinical variables but is explicitly for algorithm validation, not clinical inference; CVS reports MDS-UPDRS in a 600+ PD longitudinal cohort
mobilised.modality: TVS lower-back McRoberts MM+ IMU plus reference systems, with some wrist-worn recordings; CVS lower-back wearable worn for seven days after study visits
mobilised.verdict: Do not preregister/download for the active WearGait-PD T1/T3 CCC objective. The open TVS is an algorithm-validation dataset and not a clinical UPDRS-III regression target. CVS is potentially useful future lower-back longitudinal T3 evidence, but remains a release/access/schema watch route; no scaffold, preregistration, download, or remote job is justified until row-level wearable plus MDS-UPDRS III data access is confirmed.

route.name: TLVMC / DeFOG Parkinson's Freezing of Gait Prediction
route.access_status: public via Kaggle competition files and Zenodo archived competition dataset; iter51 full public raw-data run completed on remote
route.label: subjects.csv contains UPDRSIII_On, UPDRSIII_Off, and NFOGQ; DeFOG metadata joins recordings to Subject/Visit/Medication
route.modality: lower-back/trunk accelerometry with AccV/AccML/AccAP axes for DeFOG structured FoG tasks plus daily free-living recordings; tdcsfog metadata exists but did not join to UPDRS-III targets in this probe
route.verdict: Direct public T3 external route; iter51 zero-shot is complete. WearGait lower-back magnitude features transfer weakly but positively to OFF-state DeFOG, wrist-to-lumbar stress transfer is near-null, DeFOG-only LOSO sanity is modest, and no internal WearGait-PD T3 canonical change is allowed from this external result.

preregistration.interpretation: external transportability evidence only; no internal canonical change

skipped_raw_files.reason: Usecols do not match columns, columns expected but not found: ['Valid', 'Task']

zero_shot_result.interpretation: partial external-validity evidence only; no internal WearGait-PD T3 canonical change

primary.name: DeFOG OFF-state T3
primary.definition: Use only defog_metadata rows with Medication == 'off' and non-missing UPDRSIII_Off after Subject/Visit join.
primary.unit_of_analysis: Subject/Visit/Medication. If a future raw-data parse yields multiple files for the same unit, aggregate predictions by median before computing headline metrics.

route.name: PDFE turning-in-place
route.label: session-level UPDRS-III total in PDFEinfo.csv; session 1 used for iter52 primary external scoring
route.modality: shank IMU acceleration and gyroscope during turning-in-place; video files also public but not used
route.verdict: Direct public T3 external route; iter52 zero-shot is complete. WearGait lateral-shank transfer is negative, clinical+shank transfer is weak and CI-crossing-zero, PDFE-only LOOCV sanity is positive, and no internal WearGait-PD T3 canonical change is allowed from this external result.

preregistration.experiment: T3 iter52 PDFE turning-in-place external zero-shot validation
preregistration.primary_rows: one row per PDFE subject: trial/session 1 only, target = Session 1 UPDRS-III
preregistration.interpretation: external transportability evidence only; no internal canonical change

zero_shot_result.interpretation: external-validity evidence only; no internal WearGait-PD T3 canonical change

consults.kimi: document_only_conservative_due_shank_protocol_mismatch; empirical iter52 run recorded as external-only negative/weak transfer

public_gait_biomechanics.name: Public overground walking full-body biomechanics
public_gait_biomechanics.label: ON/OFF UPDRS-III total and item columns in PDGinfo.xlsx
public_gait_biomechanics.modality: 3D motion capture and force plates during overground walking; no wearable IMU feature space
public_gait_biomechanics.verdict: Public target-bearing gait-biomechanics dataset, but not a WearGait-PD T1/T3 CCC route because the modality is laboratory motion capture and force plates rather than wearable IMU.

route.name: Papadopoulos phone-call tremor
route.label: tremor-specific annotations only: UPDRS II item 16 plus Part III item 20/21 left/right hand tremor in the clinically examined group
route.modality: smartphone embedded IMU acceleration recorded during phone calls, in-the-wild
route.verdict: Public tremor-subitem/free-living context only. It lacks total MDS-UPDRS Part III and T1 items 9-14, uses smartphone phone-call accelerometry rather than WearGait gait/balance IMU tasks, and should not receive a preregistration, download, or remote job for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

consult.purpose: Persist external-consult evidence for the Papadopoulos phone-call tremor route decision.

route.name: Papadopoulos phone-call tremor
route.modality: smartphone embedded-IMU acceleration captured during phone calls
labels: UPDRS Part III item 20 right/left rest hand tremor
UPDRS Part III item 21 right/left action/postural hand tremor
route.eligibility_failure: No total MDS-UPDRS Part III target, no T1 items 9-14, and no WearGait-aligned gait/balance protocol.

kimi.summary: Do not launch another model experiment or access-gated scaffold until genuinely new data or credentials exist; record the negative web-search result and keep the paper/provenance story tight.

gemini.summary: Stop new modeling; formally reject the smartphone tremor dataset for this objective because it lacks total UPDRS-III labels.

consult.decision: No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job. Keep as tremor-subitem / free-living context only.

route.name: Harmonized Upper/Lower Limb Accelerometry
route.access_status: NICHD DASH controlled access Part1/Part2; processing code public on GitHub/Zenodo
route.label: demographic/clinical survey fields; no confirmed total MDS-UPDRS Part III or T1 items 9-14
route.modality: daily-life ActiGraph upper/lower-limb accelerometry and processed summary variables
route.verdict: Document-only route. The dataset is a valuable rehab/free-living accelerometry resource, but it lacks confirmed total MDS-UPDRS Part III or T1 items 9-14, is controlled-access through DASH, and exposes daily-life ActiGraph summaries rather than WearGait-aligned gait/balance raw IMU. No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job is justified for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

consult.purpose: Persist external-consult evidence for the Harmonized Upper/Lower Limb Accelerometry route decision.

route.name: Harmonized Upper/Lower Limb Accelerometry
route.modality: daily-life ActiGraph upper/lower-limb accelerometry with processed summary variables
labels: no confirmed total MDS-UPDRS Part III
route.eligibility_failure: No confirmed total MDS-UPDRS Part III or T1 items 9-14; daily-life ActiGraph summaries are not aligned to WearGait-PD structured gait/balance raw IMU features.

kimi.summary: Hard target gap is disqualifying; H&Y alone is not a T1/T3 target. Daily-life ActiGraph summaries and small PD N make the route incompatible with the current ceiling-break objective.

gemini.summary: Dataset lacks confirmed MDS-UPDRS III total or T1 subitems and has severe modality mismatch versus structured WearGait raw IMU.

consult.decision: No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job. Keep only as rehab/free-living accelerometry context.

route.name: Monipar
route.label: exercise-level MDS-UPDRS subitem labels for the supervised group only; no confirmed total Part III
route.modality: single consumer-smartwatch triaxial accelerometer at 50 Hz during eight structured exercises
route.verdict: Public consumer-smartwatch subitem dataset. It lacks total MDS-UPDRS Part III, lacks a full T1 9-14 composite, and has only six supervised PD subjects with item labels. No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job is justified for the active WearGait-PD T1/T3 CCC objective.

refresh.name: Monipar
refresh.modality: single consumer smartwatch triaxial accelerometer at 50 Hz
refresh.labels: exercise-level MDS-UPDRS subitem scores for the supervised group only; no confirmed total Part III
refresh.verdict: Public consumer-smartwatch subitem dataset. It lacks total MDS-UPDRS Part III, lacks a full T1 9-14 composite, and has only six supervised PD subjects with item labels. No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job is justified for the active WearGait-PD T1/T3 CCC objective.

route.name: BIOCLITE
route.label: per-exercise MDS-UPDRS score 0-4 when a clinical evaluation is available; no total Part III endpoint
route.modality: single consumer-smartwatch triaxial accelerometer and gyroscope at 50 Hz across supervised and unsupervised exercise sessions
route.verdict: Public smartwatch follow-up to Monipar with acc+gyro and supervised/unsupervised sessions. It is useful related work for exercise-level motor subitems, but it cannot support total T3 or the full T1 items 9-14 composite. No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job is justified for the active WearGait-PD T1/T3 CCC objective.

refresh.name: BIOCLITE
refresh.modality: single consumer smartwatch triaxial accelerometer and gyroscope at 50 Hz
refresh.labels: per-exercise MDS-UPDRS score 0-4 when a clinical evaluation is available; no total Part III endpoint
refresh.verdict: Public smartwatch follow-up to Monipar with acc+gyro and supervised/unsupervised sessions. It is useful related work for exercise-level motor subitems, but it cannot support total T3 or the full T1 items 9-14 composite. No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job is justified for the active WearGait-PD T1/T3 CCC objective.

route.name: Personalized Parkinson Project / PD Virtual Motor Exam
route.access_status: RDSRC request-gated; no local access or schema; data release fees may apply
route.label: MDS-UPDRS Part III at clinic visits and consensus subitem ratings for PD-VME validation subsets
route.modality: Verily Study Watch wrist sensors during passive monitoring and self-guided active motor tasks
route.verdict: Strong gated Verily-watch external route and useful peer to PPMI/Verily, but not public or locally approved. Do not create a scaffold, preregistration, download job, or remote run until RDSRC access is approved and row-level sensor/label schema is available.

refresh.name: Personalized Parkinson Project / PD Virtual Motor Exam
refresh.access_status: RDSRC request-gated; data release fees may apply
refresh.modality: Verily Study Watch wrist sensors during passive monitoring and PD-VME active tasks
refresh.labels: MDS-UPDRS Part III at clinic visits and consensus subitem ratings for PD-VME validation subsets
refresh.verdict: Strong gated Verily-watch external route and a useful peer to PPMI/Verily. It is not public or locally approved, so no scaffold, preregistration, download, or remote job is justified until a data request is approved and row-level schema is available.

route.name: Comprehensive Multi-Modal Dataset for Parkinson's Disease Prediction
route.label: UPDRS part totals and scalar gait summaries in a derived table; no item-level Part III raw subitems
route.modality: CSF protein/peptide features plus scalar gait summaries (gait_time, gait_steps, freezing), not raw wearable IMU
route.verdict: Public derived benchmark table, not a contemporaneous wearable sensor cohort. It mixes Kaggle AMP clinical/CSF data with a Mendeley gait repository, exposes only scalar gait summaries rather than raw IMU, and lacks T1 item-level labels or a trustworthy subject-aligned wearable-to-UPDRS schema. No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job is justified.

refresh.purpose: Fresh public-route check for Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.14848598 after the smartwatch subitem route refresh.

route.name: Comprehensive Multi-Modal Dataset for Parkinson's Disease Prediction

inspection.primary_columns: visit_id plus CSF protein/peptide features; no raw wearable IMU columns

source_lineage: AMP Parkinson's Disease Progression Prediction Data (Kaggle)
route.label: UPDRS part totals and scalar gait summaries in a derived table; no item-level Part III raw subitems
route.modality: CSF protein/peptide features plus scalar gait summaries (gait_time, gait_steps, freezing), not raw wearable IMU
route.verdict: Public derived benchmark table, not a contemporaneous wearable sensor cohort. It mixes Kaggle AMP clinical/CSF data with a Mendeley gait repository, exposes only scalar gait summaries rather than raw IMU, and lacks T1 item-level labels or a trustworthy subject-aligned wearable-to-UPDRS schema. No preregistration, download, scaffold, or remote job is justified.

advanced_smartwatch_route.name: Advanced PD smartwatch home monitoring / Fay-Karmon 2024
advanced_smartwatch_route.access_status: data available from corresponding author upon reasonable request; no local files or row-level schema
advanced_smartwatch_route.label: MDS-UPDRS Part II and Part III in ON/OFF states plus Part IV, daily motor-task labels and diaries
advanced_smartwatch_route.modality: Intel Pharma Analytics Platform smartwatch plus iPhone/app during approximately two weeks of home-based monitoring with proprietary SWA algorithm outputs
advanced_smartwatch_route.verdict: Request-only small advanced-PD smartwatch route. It is not actionable for the active T1/T3 CCC objective because the row-level files/schema are not public, SWA scoring is proprietary, N=21 is below stronger already-tested external rows, and no T1 item-level target is visible. Do not create a scaffold, preregistration, download job, or remote run unless data-owner approval and schema are obtained.

dyad_actigraphy_route.name: Marital-dyad social actigraphy / Sensors 2023
dyad_actigraphy_route.access_status: source data available to researchers upon author request; no local files or row-level schema
dyad_actigraphy_route.label: clinician MDS-UPDRS Part III total for the PD participant
dyad_actigraphy_route.modality: non-dominant wrist GeneActiv triaxial accelerometry at 100 Hz for seven days in PD participants and spouses
dyad_actigraphy_route.verdict: Request-only small daily-life/social-actigraphy route. It is not actionable for the active T1/T3 CCC objective because the row-level files/schema are not public, the protocol is dyad/social activity rather than structured WearGait-like gait/balance, N=27 PD participants is underpowered after stronger external rows, and no T1 item-level target is visible. Do not create a scaffold, preregistration, download job, or remote run unless data-owner approval and schema are obtained.

refresh.purpose: Fresh route check for two newly surfaced request-only Parkinson wearable studies with MDS-UPDRS Part III context.

notes: Fay-Karmon advanced-PD smartwatch home monitoring is N=21, request-only, proprietary Intel SWA/scoring, and schema-hidden.
Marital-dyad social actigraphy is N=27 PD dyads, request-only, daily-life/social-actigraphy rather than structured gait/balance, and dyad design complicates subject independence.
Neither route should receive a preregistration, scaffold, download, or remote job without data-owner access and row-level schema.

routes.name: Advanced PD smartwatch home monitoring / Fay-Karmon 2024
routes.access_status: data available from corresponding author upon reasonable request; no local files or schema
routes.label: MDS-UPDRS Part II and Part III in ON/OFF states plus Part IV, daily motor-task labels and symptom diaries
routes.modality: Intel Pharma Analytics Platform smartwatch plus iPhone/app during approximately two weeks of home-based monitoring with proprietary SWA algorithm outputs
routes.verdict: Potentially relevant as a small external T3/advanced-PD home-monitoring context only after data-owner approval, but not actionable for the active ceiling-break objective. N=21 is below stronger already-tested external rows, SWA algorithms are proprietary/schema-hidden, no T1 item-level route is visible, and no public row-level sensor/label files exist.

routes.name: Marital-dyad social actigraphy / Sensors 2023
routes.access_status: source data available to researchers upon author request; no local files or schema
routes.label: clinician MDS-UPDRS Part III total for the PD participant
routes.modality: non-dominant wrist GeneActiv triaxial accelerometry at 100 Hz for seven days in PD participants and spouses
routes.verdict: Potentially relevant as small request-only daily-life actigraphy context, but not actionable for the active ceiling-break objective. The study is N=27 PD participants, social/dyad-focused, not a structured WearGait-like gait/balance protocol, has no T1 item-level endpoint, and exposes no public row-level sensor/label files.

route.name: Luxembourg upper-limb MDS-UPDRS III subitem study
route.access_status: paper public; datasets not publicly available and only available upon reasonable request under national/institutional regulations
route.label: MDS-UPDRS III upper-limb subitems; no total Part III endpoint for current objective
route.modality: two compact hand IMUs during elicited upper-limb tasks
route.verdict: Document-only upper-limb observability context. The route maps to residual-burden items 4/5/6, but it is request-only, ON-medication, subitem-only, and not a total T3 or full T1 endpoint. Do not create an access runbook, preregistration, scaffold, download job, or remote run for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

refresh.route: Luxembourg / NCER-PD Sensors 2024 upper-limb MDS-UPDRS III subitem IMU study
rationale: The public paper describes 33 PD patients and 12 controls, six elicited hand/arm MDS-UPDRS III tasks, and bilateral compact hand IMUs.
The route targets upper-limb subitems, not total MDS-UPDRS Part III and not the full T1 items 9-14 composite.
The data are not publicly downloadable; the data-availability statement says datasets are available only upon reasonable request under national and institutional regulations.

The current T3 residual audits already identify upper-limb items as non-WearGait-observable burden, so this route supports the observability-ceiling argument rather than creating a WearGait-PD internal ceiling-break path.
Prior per-item T3 composition failed at this N, so external upper-limb subitem data do not escape the known variance-compounding failure mode.

consults.kimi: skip_runbook_document_only; use as upper-limb observability context only

methodology_caveats: Medication state is ON in the public Sensors 2024 study.
The public paper does not expose row-level raw files or schema.
The task and sensor geometry are bilateral hand IMUs during elicited upper-limb tasks, not WearGait-PD gait/balance wrist/ankle/lumbar recordings.
No preregistration, download, scaffold, remote job, or request packet is justified for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

route.name: Pre-QuantiPark / ActiMyo levodopa-challenge wearable IMU pilot
route.access_status: paper public; data request-gated for academic non-commercial use after written proposal review and data access agreement
route.label: Repeated MDS-UPDRS Part III total during a 90-minute levodopa challenge; no visible T1 items 9-14 endpoint
route.modality: ActiMyo magneto-inertial sensors on the most affected wrist and ankle, 130.69 Hz, controlled levodopa-challenge setting
route.verdict: Document-only levodopa-challenge wearable-IMU context. The route has repeated MDS-UPDRS Part III during a within-subject L-dopa challenge, but it is request-only, N=10, highly selected, schema-hidden, and not a stable cross-sectional WearGait-PD T1/T3 ceiling-break route. Do not create an access runbook, preregistration, scaffold, download job, request packet, or remote run for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

refresh.route: Pre-QuantiPark / ActiMyo levodopa-challenge wearable IMU pilot
rationale: The public Scientific Reports 2025 paper describes only 10 PD patients undergoing a 90-minute levodopa challenge test, not a stable cross-sectional WearGait-PD severity cohort.
The data are not public-download; access may be granted only for academic non-commercial research after written proposal review and a data access agreement.

MDS-UPDRS Part III total scores were collected repeatedly during the challenge, so a T3-style endpoint is possible in principle only after access and schema review, but a subject-level N=10 external CCC would be dominated by variance.
The route does not visibly expose a full T1 items 9-14 label path, and no row-level raw files or schema are locally available.
At N=10, a subject-level 5-fold screen would have about two subjects per fold, so it cannot satisfy the repo's promotion/lockbox gate before any LOOCV or zero-shot claim.

consults.kimi: document_only_no_runbook_no_prereg_no_scaffold; N=10 and levodopa-challenge design make a lockbox incoherent

methodology_caveats: The public protocol is a single-dose levodopa challenge with repeated within-subject ratings, not a one-row-per-subject cross-sectional severity endpoint.
Sensors are ActiMyo units on the most affected wrist and ankle, sampled at 130.69 Hz.
Participants were DBS candidates with motor fluctuations and dyskinesia, so the cohort is highly selected.
No access runbook, preregistration, download, scaffold, remote job, or request packet is justified for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

route.name: TUM Donie 2025 ROCKET/InceptionTime wrist symptom-classification alias
route.access_status: paper and code public; underlying data are MJFF Levodopa Response syn20681023 and remain Synapse DUA-gated
route.label: Task-level tremor severity plus bradykinesia/dyskinesia presence labels; not total MDS-UPDRS Part III and not T1 items 9-14
route.modality: GENEActiv smartwatch/wrist accelerometer on most affected limb, 50 Hz, predefined motor tasks
route.verdict: Document-only alias to the existing Hssayeni/MJFF DUA gate and already-negative ROCKET/InceptionTime-style algorithm families. The public code is useful related work, but the data source is still syn20681023, the labels are task-level symptom classifications rather than T1/T3 regression targets, and local ROCKET/MultiROCKET plus learned time-series fine-tuning routes have already failed. Do not create an access runbook, clone/scaffold, preregistration, download job, or remote run for the active T1/T3 CCC objective.

refresh.route: TUM Donie 2025 ROCKET/InceptionTime wrist symptom-classification alias
rationale: The Scientific Reports 2025 paper uses a 27-patient subset of MJFF Levodopa Response Study syn20681023, which is already represented locally as the Hssayeni/MJFF route and remains Synapse DUA-gated.
The public endpoint is task-level symptom classification for tremor severity plus bradykinesia and dyskinesia presence/absence, not WearGait-PD T1 items 9-14 or total MDS-UPDRS Part III regression.
The paper's public code does not remove the data-access gate; its README still requires each user to download Synapse data with credentials.
The relevant algorithm families have already been tested or closed locally: ROCKET/MultiROCKET Stage-2 routes were negative, and small-N learned time-series fine-tuning failed in the HARNet pilot.
No leak-free lockbox path exists without DUA-approved data, a compatible T1/T3 target, and a non-negative 5-fold gate.

consults.kimi: document_only_alias_no_scaffold; same syn20681023 DUA gate, target mismatch, ROCKET/InceptionTime family already exhausted

methodology_caveats: The source study is classification-oriented, using balanced accuracy, average precision, AUROC, F1, and macro-averaged MAE; it is not a CCC regression benchmark.
A public code repository is useful related work, but cloning or porting it would not create an eligible WearGait-PD T1/T3 route before Synapse DUA approval and target/schema inspection.

The paper itself reports that InceptionTime/ROCKET do not reach clinically relevant performance and that customized manual features can still outperform automated time-series classifiers for PD symptom severity prediction.

hssayeni.name: MJFF Levodopa Response / Hssayeni
hssayeni.label: MDS-UPDRS and symptom labels
hssayeni.verdict: Larger direct external wearable UPDRS-III route, but blocked until DUA approval.
hssayeni.alias_note: Scientific Data 2021 minimum-set and limb/trunk accelerometry papers are the same Synapse syn20681023 MJFF/Hssayeni route, not separate unblocked datasets.
